mtkruto.question: How does calling the high-level client.sendMessage() method get the message serialized into a TL/MTProto request and sent over the network transport to Telegram's servers? Trace the call path.
mtkruto.truth:
Verified call path (small TS Telegram-client lib):
1. Client.sendMessage â€” client/6_client.ts:1852 â†’ calls this.#messageManager.sendMessage(...) (1853)
2. MessageManager.sendMessage â€” client/3_message_manager.ts:330 â†’ builds the TL function {_:"messages.sendMessage", peer, message,...} and calls this.#c.invoke(...) (~377)
3. c.invoke closure / Client.#invoke â€” client/6_client.ts:279/887 â†’ resolves a ClientEncrypted and calls client.invoke(function_) (897)
4. ClientEncrypted.invoke â€” client/2_client_encrypted.ts:324 â†’ this.#send(function_) (325)
5. ClientEncrypted.#send â€” client/2_client_encrypted.ts:261 â†’ SERIALIZES via Api.serializeObject(function_) (290), then this.session.send(body) (296)
   - Serialization: Api.serializeObject (tl/2_telegram.ts:38) â†’ new TLWriter().writeObject(...) (tl/1_tl_writer.ts) writes constructor id + fields
6. SessionEncrypted.send â€” session/2_session_encrypted.ts:193 â†’ ENQUEUES a PendingMessage and wakes the send loop (#awakeSendLoop) [DYNAMIC: async queue, not a direct call]
7. SessionEncrypted.#sendLoopBody (AbortableLoop) â€” session/2_session_encrypted.ts:282 â†’ #encryptMessage (serializeMessage tl/2_message.ts + IGE-256 AES) then this.transport.transport.send(payload) (339)
8. Transport.send â€” TransportAbridged.send (transport/1_transport_abridged.ts:71) or TransportIntermediate â†’ this.#connection.write(encrypt(framed))
9. ConnectionTCP.write (connection/1_connection_tcp.ts:96) or ConnectionWebSocket.write â€” bytes exit the process.
KEY SYMBOLS a correct answer must hit: sendMessage â†’ MessageManager.sendMessage â†’ invoke (ClientEncrypted.invoke/#send) â†’ Api.serializeObject/TLWriter â†’ SessionEncrypted.send (queue) â†’ #sendLoopBody/#encryptMessage â†’ Transport.send â†’ Connection.write.
DYNAMIC BOUNDARIES: the invoke indirection (closureâ†’#invokeâ†’ClientEncrypted), and the send-loop QUEUE between SessionEncrypted.send and #sendLoopBody (async, no direct edge).

postybirb.question: How does submitting/queueing a post reach the website-specific code that actually uploads to a target website? Trace the path from the submission entry point in the NestJS server to a concrete website service's post implementation.
postybirb.truth:
Verified call path (NestJS+Electron, server under electron-app/src/server/):
1. Entry: PostController.queue (submission/post/post.controller.ts:33, POST queue/:id) OR SubmissionService.queueScheduledSubmissions (submission/submission.service.ts, @Interval(60000) scheduler) â€” both call PostService.queue
2. PostService.queue â€” submission/post/post.service.ts:68 â†’ this.post(submission)
3. PostService.post (private) â€” post.service.ts:206 â†’ this.createPoster(...) for each non-default SubmissionPart
4. PostService.createPoster â€” post.service.ts:373 â†’ website = this.websites.getWebsiteModule(part.website) [part.website is a STRING]
5. WebsiteProvider.getWebsiteModule â€” websites/website-provider.service.ts:86 â†’ websiteModulesMap[name.toLowerCase()] [DYNAMIC: string-keyed registry of DI-injected Website singletons]; then new Poster(..., website, ...)
6. Poster constructor â€” submission/post/poster.ts:117 â†’ setTimeout(this.post.bind(this), delay) [DYNAMIC: async timer, min ~5s]
7. Poster.post (poster.ts:131) â†’ performPost (171) â†’ attemptPost (217)
8. Poster.attemptPost â€” poster.ts:217 â†’ this.website.postFileSubmission(token, data, accountData) [DYNAMIC: polymorphic dispatch on abstract Website] (or postNotificationSubmission)
9. Website.postFileSubmission (abstract) â€” websites/website.base.ts:102
10. Concrete e.g. FurAffinity.postFileSubmission â€” websites/fur-affinity/fur-affinity.service.ts:231 â†’ multi-step HTTP upload (GET /submit, POST /submit/upload multipart, POST /submit/finalize)
KEY SYMBOLS: PostService.queue â†’ PostService.post â†’ createPoster â†’ WebsiteProvider.getWebsiteModule (registry) â†’ Poster (setTimeout) â†’ Poster.attemptPost â†’ Website.postFileSubmission (abstract base) â†’ a concrete website service (e.g. FurAffinity).
DYNAMIC BOUNDARIES: NestJS DI builds the website registry; string-keyed map lookup; setTimeout defers Poster.post; polymorphic dispatch on the abstract Website base. A correct answer must reach a concrete website's post via the registry + base class, not stop at PostService.

shapeshift.question: How does executing a swap work in ShapeShift â€” from the code that fetches quotes and selects a swapper down to a specific swapper's execute/trade? Name the swapper interface, the registry, and one concrete swapper, and trace the path.
shapeshift.truth:
Verified call path (large multi-package monorepo; swap logic in packages/swapper + execution in src/lib):
QUOTE/REGISTRY layer:
- Swapper interface = the `Swapper` type (execute methods) + `SwapperApi` type (getTradeQuote/getUnsignedTx) â€” packages/swapper/src/types.ts (~846/897)
- Registry = `swappers: Record<SwapperName,(SwapperApi & Swapper)>` â€” packages/swapper/src/constants.ts:52 (merges e.g. thorchainSwapper + thorchainApi)
- Aggregator: swapperApi RTK endpoint getTradeQuote/getTradeRates (src/state/apis/swapper/swapperApi.ts:78/156) â†’ getTradeQuotes (packages/swapper/src/swapper.ts:18/26): swapper = swappers[swapperName]; swapper.getTradeQuote(...) [DYNAMIC interface dispatch]
- Concrete (THORChain): thorchainApi.getTradeQuote (swappers/ThorchainSwapper/endpoints.ts â†’ getTradeQuote/getTradeQuote.ts:15) â†’ getL1RateOrQuote â†’ getQuote (thorService HTTP)
SELECTION: tradeQuoteSlice selectors rank quotes (selectSortedTradeQuotes / selectActiveSwapperName) [DYNAMIC: winner chosen by ranking/user]
EXECUTION layer:
- useTradeExecution (src/components/MultiHopTrade/.../hooks/useTradeExecution.tsx:200/476) â†’ new TradeExecution(); execution.execEvmTransaction(...) (CowSwap: execEvmMessage)
- TradeExecution.execEvmTransaction â€” src/lib/tradeExecution.ts:326 â†’ _execWalletAgnostic(...) (372)
- TradeExecution._execWalletAgnostic â€” tradeExecution.ts:136 â†’ swapper = swappers[swapperName] (149) [DYNAMIC registry]; buildSignBroadcast â†’ swapper.getUnsignedEvmTransaction(...) (355) then swapper.executeEvmTransaction(unsignedTx, {signAndBroadcastTransaction}) (367) [DYNAMIC SwapperApi/Swapper interface]
- Concrete (THORChain): thorchainSwapper.executeEvmTransaction (swappers/ThorchainSwapper/ThorchainSwapper.ts â†’ utils.ts:181) delegates to callbacks.signAndBroadcastTransaction (wallet). CowSwap alt: cowSwapper.executeEvmMessage â†’ signCowOrder + cowService.post.
KEY SYMBOLS: Swapper/SwapperApi types, swappers registry (constants.ts), getTradeQuotes (swapper.ts), TradeExecution._execWalletAgnostic, swapper.getUnsignedEvmTransaction/executeEvmTransaction, one concrete swapper (thorchainSwapper/zrxSwapper/cowSwapper).
DYNAMIC BOUNDARIES: swappers[name] registry lookup (2 sites); all hops into a concrete swapper are via the Swapper/SwapperApi interface, never a direct function. A correct answer must name the interface + registry and reach a concrete swapper through interface dispatch.

trezor.question: How does sending a crypto transaction flow from the send form's review/sign action through to signing it via @trezor/connect (TrezorConnect.signTransaction)? Trace the call path.
trezor.truth:
Verified call path (trezor-suite monorepo; app in packages/suite, shared logic in suite-common/wallet-core, device API in packages/connect):
1. ReviewButton.handleButtonReviewClick â€” packages/suite/src/views/wallet/send/TotalSent/ReviewButton.tsx:120 â†’ signTransaction() (= useSendForm's sign)
2. useSendForm.sign (exported as signTransaction) â€” packages/suite/src/hooks/wallet/useSendForm.ts:278 â†’ dispatch(signAndPushSendFormTransactionThunk({formState, precomposedTransaction, selectedAccount})) [DYNAMIC: redux thunk]
3. signAndPushSendFormTransactionThunk â€” packages/suite/src/actions/wallet/send/sendFormThunks.ts:237 â†’ (first enhancePrecomposedTransactionThunk) then dispatch(signTransactionThunk({...})) [cross-package: thunk from suite-common/wallet-core]
4. signTransactionThunk (coin-routing hub) â€” suite-common/wallet-core/src/send/sendFormThunks.ts:532/573 â†’ networkType branch â†’ dispatch(signBitcoinSendFormTransactionThunk) (ethereumâ†’signEthereumSendFormTransactionThunk, etc.) [DYNAMIC: runtime coin dispatch]
5. signBitcoinSendFormTransactionThunk â€” suite-common/wallet-core/src/send/sendFormBitcoinThunks.ts:394 â†’ await TrezorConnect.signTransaction(signPayload)
6. TrezorConnect.signTransaction (facade) â€” packages/connect-common/src/factory.ts â†’ closure calls impl.call({method:'signTransaction',...}) [DYNAMIC: facade / iframe-or-module boundary]
7. CoreInModule.call â€” packages/connect/src/impl/core-in-module.ts:171 â†’ posts CORE_CALL to core (deferred promise)
8. Core.onCall â†’ getMethod â€” packages/connect/src/core/index.ts â†’ getMethod resolves 'signTransaction' â†’ new SignTransaction(message) [DYNAMIC: nameâ†’class]
9. SignTransaction.run â€” packages/connect/src/api/signTransaction.ts:317 â†’ signTx via device.getCommands().typedCall (protobuf to device). After signing, signAndPushSendFormTransactionThunk â†’ pushSendFormTransactionThunk â†’ TrezorConnect.pushTransaction.
KEY SYMBOLS: ReviewButton â†’ useSendForm.sign â†’ signAndPushSendFormTransactionThunk (suite) â†’ signTransactionThunk (wallet-core, coin hub) â†’ signBitcoinSendFormTransactionThunk â†’ TrezorConnect.signTransaction â†’ connect factory/CoreInModule.call â†’ SignTransaction.run.
DYNAMIC BOUNDARIES: every suiteâ†’wallet-core hop is a redux thunk dispatch; signTransactionThunk branches by networkType at runtime; TrezorConnect is a dynamically-built facade crossing an iframe/module boundary; the SignTransaction class is resolved by name. A correct answer must cross suiteâ†’wallet-coreâ†’connect and reach TrezorConnect.signTransaction / SignTransaction.run, not stop at the UI.
